rationale: This supervisor structure prioritizes safe, efficient exploration by preferring forward movement in clear conditions, using deterministic single-action recovery when obstacles are detected, and employing helper commit states to ensure one-shot actions. It avoids oscillations by assigning a unique rotation direction for each side obstacle and uses full scans only when necessary. All uncontrollable events are handled in every state, and controllable actions are never enabled in parallel except where justified for recovery. Helper states ensure that action execution and perception are cleanly separated.
strategy: Prefer forward movement in clear state to maximize coverage.
Upon detecting an obstacle in front, perform a full scan to find a new direction.
If an obstacle is detected on the left, rotate clockwise to avoid it.
If an obstacle is detected on the right, rotate counterclockwise to avoid it.
Use commit states to ensure actions are executed as atomic steps and always return to a perception state.
All uncontrollable events are handled in every state to ensure safety and determinism.
No oscillatory behavior: only one rotation direction is enabled per side obstacle.
Full scan is used only when forward is blocked, not for side obstacles.
No infinite controllable loops or parallel controllable actions.
Helper states (commit/scan) ensure clean separation between action and perception.
